enforced.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject (raise error/crash) or at minimum tag REJECT; silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: SEQ: segment SEQuence. This field can be a `*' when the sequence is not stored. If not a `*', the length of the sequence must equal the sum of lengths of MIS=X operations in CIGAR.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM optional tag field ordering invariance
quarantine.oracle: The parsed alignment records should be semantically equivalent regardless of the order of optional TAG:TYPE:VALUE fields in columns 12+

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.
Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.oracle: Parsed @HD header must be semantically equivalent after shuffling TAG:VALUE pairs

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.oracle: Parsed @SQ reference sequence records must be semantically equivalent after shuffling TAG:VALUE pairs within each line

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.oracle: Parsed @RG read-group records must be semantically equivalent after shuffling TAG:VALUE pairs within each line

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.oracle: Parsed @PG program records must be semantically equivalent after shuffling TAG:VALUE pairs within each line

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: Flag-bit exclusivity violation: mapped read with 0x4 unmapped flag
quarantine.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject (raise error/crash) or at minimum tag REJECT; silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p137
evidence.quote: * If all segments in a template are unmapped, their RNAME should be set as `*' and POS as 0.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

evidence.quote: Bit 0x4 is the only reliable place to tell whether the read is unmapped. If 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, and bits 0x2, 0x100, and 0x800.

ambiguity_flags: The spec uses 'should' for unmapped RNAME/POS recommendations, but the flag-bit contradiction creates logical inconsistency that should be rejected

quarantine.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject (raise error/crash) or at minimum tag REJECT; silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: Predefined tags are described in the separate Sequence Alignment/Map Optional Fields Specification.See http://samtools.github.io/hts-specs/SAMtags.pdf SAMtags.pdf at https://github.com/samtools/hts-specs.

ambiguity_flags: The exact type character restrictions are in the separate SAMtags specification, but illegal type characters should be rejected

quarantine.mr_name: TLEN sign consistency violation for paired reads
quarantine.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject (raise error/crash) or at minimum tag REJECT; silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p136
evidence.quote: * For a unmapped paired-end or mate-pair read whose mate is mapped, the unmapped read should have RNAME and POS identical to its mate.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec describes TLEN sign conventions but doesn't explicitly forbid same-signed TLENs; however, opposite signs are required for proper paired-end representation

quarantine.mr_name: Coordinate wrapping invariance for circular references
quarantine.oracle: When POS exceeds reference length LN in circular reference sequences (TP:circular), coordinate wrapping should preserve alignment semantics and query method results should remain invariant

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p145
evidence.quote:
* (Preferred)
As usual POS should be between 1 and the @SQ header's LN value, but POS plus the sum of the lengths of M=XDN CIGAR operations may exceed LN.
Coordinates greater than LN are interpreted by subtracting LN so that bases at +1, +2, +3, … are considered to be mapped at positions 1,2,3,…; thus each (1-based) position p is interpreted as ((p-1))+1.
[The impact of this representation on indexing and random access is yet to be explored by implementations.]
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec uses 'should' (ADVISORY) not 'must' (CRITICAL) for coordinate wrapping behavior
Impact on indexing and random access is explicitly noted as unexplored

quarantine.mr_name: SAM CIGAR adjacent operation merge/split invariance
quarantine.oracle: Parsed canonical representation must be identical after splitting or merging adjacent identical CIGAR operations

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p134
evidence.quote: * Adjacent CIGAR operations should be different.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

quarantine.mr_name: Optional tag field permutation invariance for query methods
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: Header TAG:VALUE pair ordering invariance for query methods
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SQ record TAG:VALUE pair ordering invariance for query methods
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.oracle: Parsed header should be semantically identical after shuffling @CO comment lines

evidence.quote: In the header, each line is TAB-delimited and, apart from  @CO lines, each data field follows a format ` TAG:VALUE' where  TAG is a two-character string that defines the format and content of  VALUE.

ambiguity_flags: The spec distinguishes @CO lines from other header lines but doesn't explicitly state ordering semantics; however, as free-text comments they have no ordering requirements

quarantine.oracle: SAM uses 1-based coordinate system with closed intervals; API methods that return coordinates should consistently use this system regardless of header field ordering

evidence.quote: [0-based coordinate system] A coordinate system where the first base of a sequence is zero. In this coordinate system, a region is specified by a half-closed-half-open interval. For example, the region between the 3rd and the 7th bases inclusive is $[2,7)$. The BAM, BCFv2, BED, and PSL formats are using the 0-based coordinate system.

1-based coordinate system A coordinate system where the first base of a sequence is one. In this coordinate system, a region is specified by a closed interval. For example, the region between the 3rd and the 7th bases inclusive is [3,7]. The SAM, VCF, GFF and Wiggle formats are using the 1-based coordinate system.

quarantine.mr_name: CIGAR operator splitting/merging invariance under query methods
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method

evidence.quote: * Adjacent CIGAR operations should be different.
